summary: Misdated July 11 1970 midnight Fillmore East concert with mostly New Riders of the Purple Sage material; recording quality uneven and many reviewers disappointed by lack of Grateful Dead content.
review: The tape appears to be from a July 11, 1970 Fillmore East midnight show, but most of the performance is New Riders of the Purple Sage rather than the Grateful Dead, which left several listeners dissatisfied. Sound quality is described as uneven – acceptable on the acoustic portion but harsh and nerve‑racking during the electric set – and the source type (soundboard vs audience) is not clearly identified.

ai_rating.rationale: Two of three reviews give zero stars, citing wrong material and poor sound; one review is a 5‑star correction note. Combined show and recording quality are low, yielding an overall rating below average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Acoustic tracks are passable, but the electric portion is described as harsh and nerve‑racking. No clear indication whether this is a soundboard or audience capture.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Midnight start with an acoustic set followed by a performance dominated by New Riders of the Purple Sage, resulting in a disjointed flow for fans expecting a full Grateful Dead show.
